items_reviewed.inventory_correction: Inventory classified as file (120B text/plain). CORRECTION: f15z1_post_z3 is a DIRECTORY containing 4 TSV files (130 lines total, ~16KB).
items_reviewed.first_commit: 5125c4d3 Capture pre-rebase legacy docs and audit history (lapidarium triage)
items_reviewed.content_summary: Same structure as f15z1 but captures post-Z3 state. Key diff vs f15z1: scripts show exit=0 with status=*_passed/*_ready/*_completed instead of exit=1 with status=*_warn/*_blocked. Tests show exit=0 instead of exit=1 for g16-g19/h1/i1-i7 tests. Represents the progression from Zone-1 to post-Zone-3 in F15 execution.
items_reviewed.gate_dependency: None detected. Content is execution snapshot output, not a control-flow marker.
items_reviewed.source_code_imports: None detected in src/, scripts/, tests/ directories.
items_reviewed.artifact_references: Referenced as path listings in lapidarium inventory artifacts — all as historical inventory entries, not live dependencies.
items_reviewed.is_unique_record: Partially. f15z1_post_z3 captures the post-Z3 delta state relative to f15z1, showing which scripts/tests transitioned from blocked/warn to ready/passed. However, this information is also derivable from the Project_ARIS git history and F15 phase artifacts, which are the authoritative records.
items_reviewed.classification_rationale: Confirmed as historical test run snapshot of F15 post-Z3 state. No source imports, no live artifact dependencies beyond inventory listings. Git history (commit 5125c4d3) preserves the content. The F15 phase artifacts in artifacts/f15/ are the authoritative record of F15 execution outcomes. Safe to remove after operator authorization.

items_reviewed.content_summary: Legacy TTS implementation using Google Genai + Piper backend. Imports: google.genai, soundfile, dotenv. Uses hardcoded paths (MODEL_PATH, PIPER_BIN). ~130 lines. This is a pre-refactor snapshot of TTS functionality.
items_reviewed.active_tts_module_imports: os, importlib.util, re, shutil, subprocess, tempfile, time, sys, threading, dataclasses, pathlib, src.aris.config.settings, src.aris.config.secrets, src.aris.external_boundary, src.aris.logging, src.aris.persona, src.aris.security.local_boundary
items_reviewed.backup_vs_active_diff: Substantial refactor: backup is ~130 lines with google.genai/soundfile/dotenv; active module is 698 lines with full ARIS settings, secrets, logging, persona, external_boundary, security integrations. These are functionally different versions of TTS.
items_reviewed.is_unique_tts_record: No. The active src/aris/voice/tts.py is the current TTS implementation. The backup is a legacy pre-refactor snapshot. Git history preserves both versions (commits 49343a05 and dcf6da5f).
items_reviewed.source_code_imports_of_backup: None. No file imports legacy/wake/tts.py.backup — it has a .backup extension and is not importable as a Python module.
items_reviewed.artifact_references: Referenced in lapidarium inventory artifacts (ARCHITECTURE_STATUS.md, PROJECT_CONTEXT_ARIS.md, cartografia, triagem, f17 artifacts) as a known legacy file. All references are documentary, not functional.
items_reviewed.classification_rationale: Active TTS is at src/aris/voice/tts.py (698 lines, fully integrated). The backup is a legacy pre-refactor snapshot that cannot be imported (.backup extension). Git history preserves the content at commits 49343a05 and dcf6da5f. The previous concern 'may be only copy of TTS code' is resolved: an active, evolved TTS module exists in src/. Backup adds no unique value. Safe to remove after operator authorization.
